title: Bug 3: Unguarded Quit button collapses whole game on any disconnect + new game deals 9 cards
description:
VERBATIM USER REPORT:
"when that waiting screen comes up... there is a quit button on that waiting screen. If by accident someone clicks on that quit button, they immediately drop the game. That quit button should not be present, and the waiting screen also should not be present. Sanika, who was the host, was getting the waiting screen and accidentally clicked the quit button. We started a new game with a new code because the host had dropped, so neither me nor Aman could do anything else after that. But the even bigger bug is: we started the new game, and in the new game I got nine cards somehow at the beginning. You can never have more than seven cards in hand. I get five cards initially and then I pick up two cards — I can never get nine cards starting a new game."

RECON LEADS (not a diagnosis — verify independently):

Part A — quit collapses whole room:
- worker/index.js (Cloudflare Durable Object RoomDO, full 45-line file, lives at project-root /worker/index.js — NOTE: CONTEXT.md says src/worker/index.js, that doc is stale/wrong path): dumb relay with NO host concept server-side. webSocketClose (lines 27-32) broadcasts PLAYER_LEFT to every remaining client on ANY disconnect — no host-migration or role logic at all. "Host" is purely a client-side flag (mpModeRef.current === 'host' in App.jsx).
- GameScreen.jsx lines 109-111: waiting screen's Quit button has zero confirmation step — one misclick triggers onHome immediately.
- App.jsx PLAYER_LEFT handler (~line 91): trims roster to [prev[0]] and sets a generic error for ANY leave (host or guest), no distinction.

Part B — 9 cards at start of new game (exact arithmetic lead):
- gameLogic.js startTurn() (lines 261-277): drawCount = player.hand.length === 0 ? 5 : 2.
- gameLogic.js initGame() (lines 20-53, deal at lines 34-36) already deals 5 cards/player up front.
- 5 (initGame) + 2 (one startTurn call) + 2 (a second startTurn call before any card played) = 9 — exact match.
- Only START_TURN dispatch site project-wide: GameScreen.jsx lines 212-218, an UNGUARDED button (no disabled state, no debounce) shown during PHASE.DRAW.
- Candidate double-dispatch triggers needing dev-team verification (NOT confirmed): double-tap on unguarded button; OR guest-to-host GAME_ACTION relay (App.jsx lines 102-104, 150-151) combined with useMultiplayer.js reconnect message-queue-flush (lines 25-70) delivering/applying the same action twice around the disconnect-to-new-room transition.

Need independent root-cause analysis + fix proposal for BOTH parts + 3-way sign-off before implementation. Highest priority — this fully blocked play.
close_reason: Shipped in commits bf2bd4f, c2a9687, fcd8a25

title: Fix JSN privacy leak when device is passed
description: GameScreen PassDeviceModal only checks currentPlayerIndex. When phase is ACTION_RESPONSE, passConfirmed is not reset, so Player 1 sees Player 2's card. Fix state reset logic.

title: Redesign all cards with official Indian edition PNG images
description: Redesign every card in the Monopoly Deal Indian Edition React app to use PNG images that mirror the official Monopoly Deal card design but with Indian-themed content. Re-wire Card.jsx to use these images, and handle fallbacks.
close_reason: Redesigned all cards using dynamically compiled PNG images with a robust procedural fallback in Card.jsx and updated the HomeScreen.

title: Bug 2: Opponent property cards show count-only, no pure/wild detail — full table redesign needed
description:
VERBATIM USER REPORT:
"the card sizes currently are very big... I am not able to show the full cards that each player is putting down in his property area. I am just showing a summarized view of how many blue cards, pink cards, orange or green cards, is it 1x3 or 2x3? When I click on that small card I can see the total rent on that set, but I still can't see how that two orange cards was made — were those two pure orange cards, or one orange + one wild property card (orange/pink)? That insight is missing. We will need to redesign the table so we can show the full cards of all six players properly, utilizing the full space of a screen like iPhone 13/14. I should be able to see all the cards laid down in the property table clearly — pure vs wild. I see that for my own properties but not for any fellow players. ... I want all the cards properly scaled up or down so all six players' cards can be seen clearly, and the X/Y positioning of the center card on the action pile also needs to be redesigned and rethought to design the final better table layout."

RECON LEADS (not a diagnosis — verify independently):
- src/components/game/PlayerBoard.jsx lines 116-184 (compact branch, used for ALL opponents): properties rendered as colored count chips only (cards.length/needed), with a RentInfoDialog on click — no per-card faces, no pure-vs-wild insight.
- lines 186-366 (full branch, local player only): lines 285-306 render actual per-card faces via <Card card={c} mini /> per color group — already shows the exact composition the user wants, just not for opponents.
- GameScreen.jsx forces `compact` for every opponent at two call sites (line 153 spectator view; lines 328-330 main play phase).
- GameScreen.jsx table-layout regions needing redesign: AppBar (~293-317), opponent strip (~321-331), center play-zone/discard pile X/Y (~333-386).
- Related prior lesson in RULES.md (2026-06-20): "Any place showing a hand card must reuse <Card card={card} mini showValue />" — same family of bug (summarized view instead of real card faces).

Need root-cause/design proposal for full 6-player table redesign on phone screen (iPhone 13/14 reference) + 3-way sign-off before implementation.
close_reason: Shipped in commits bf2bd4f, c2a9687, fcd8a25

title: Bug 1: Disruptive full-screen waiting screen during other players' actions
description:
VERBATIM USER REPORT:
"whenever one of the players is asking for rent from someone or is asking to sly a card from someone else, or is asking to force a deal with a card with some other player... three players were playing this time: me, Sanika, and Aman. When Sanika asked rent from Aman, I started getting that waiting screen which said 'Sanika ki bari hai.' That waiting screen took my focus away from the main table, and started showing me a loading screen as a separate waiting screen because of which my focus broke from what I was doing. I was looking at all the other players' cards and making my own strategy."

RECON LEADS (not a diagnosis — verify independently):
- src/components/screens/GameScreen.jsx lines 19-29: SPECTATOR_PHASES array — phases that get a softer non-blocking view instead of the full-screen block.
- lines 31-52: getActiveInteractorIdx(state) — resolves who must act right now.
- lines 96-114: the full-screen waiting block. Fires whenever `isMultiplayer && activeInteractorIdx !== myPlayerIndex && !SPECTATOR_PHASES.includes(state.phase)`. Only excludes the *target* of the action, not the asker or other uninvolved 3rd players — so any uninvolved player still gets the full-screen block.
- lines 116-198: an existing "SPECTATOR VIEW" already does this better (AppBar + compact opponent strip + own board, no full-screen takeover) but is only wired up for SPECTATOR_PHASES.
- line 17: PAYMENT_PHASES constant is declared but unused — possibly intended for this exact fix and never wired up.

Need root-cause analysis + fix proposal + 3-way sign-off (Sonu/Aman/Sanika) before implementation.
close_reason: Shipped in commits bf2bd4f, c2a9687, fcd8a25

title: Hand cards render as plain text in ActionModal payment/select sheets
description: When resolving a payment with no cash or selecting properties to steal/swap, the payer/actor's own hand displays as plain text boxes without color. Root: PlayerContextView + PaymentSheet hand-card blocks use Typography instead of reusing <Card> component. Acceptance: (1) PaymentSheet 'Haath mein' hand renders as <Card mini showValue /> with full color/type. (2) PlayerContextView in Sly Deal/Forced Deal/Sabotage/Deal Breaker sheets shows colored cards too. (3) Mini cards wrapped (not horizontal scroll). (4) No behavior regression in give-as-payment logic. (5) CounterpartyStrip/PlayerBoard unchanged.
